patch.value:
## Charter
Make the SDK the single public API of pm. Ordering: (1) inventory the CLI-to-core call graph, (2) promote primitives domain-by-domain into src/sdk with stable typed signatures, (3) flip CLI handlers to consume the SDK, (4) enforce the boundary with a lint/CI gate so it can never regress, (5) prove universality with an SDK-only exemplar consumer.

## Layering target
- src/core: private implementation detail (no stability promise)
- src/sdk: the ONLY public programmatic surface — re-exports/wraps core with typed, documented, semver-meaningful contracts
- src/cli + src/mcp: presentation layers that import exclusively from src/sdk
- packages/*: already consume the SDK; gain access to the same primitives the CLI uses

## Non-goals
- No behavior changes to any command (pure re-layering)
- No new storage formats
- Extension/package authoring SDK (compose/define/runtime/testing) stays as-is; this epic adds the missing primitive layer beside it

## Relationship to CLI simplification epic pm-n7rr
pm-n7rr consolidates the command surface; this epic supplies the primitives those consolidated commands call. Inventory (child task) feeds both.
SDK-first architecture: pm CLI (and MCP) become thin layers over a complete public pm SDK

Promote every PM primitive the CLI uses (item lifecycle, queries, annotations, governance, schema/config/profile, history) from src/core internals into the public pm SDK, then rebuild CLI command handlers as thin presentation shims over that SDK so any integrator can build a fully custom PM tool from the SDK alone. Duplicate check: searched SDK-first/boundary/public-primitives items — existing SDK work (pm-ugqx lineage) covers extension authoring only; no open overlap on core-primitive promotion.

Public SDK exposes item lifecycle, query/read, annotation/link, governance/validation, schema/config/profile, and history primitives with typed results; src/cli/commands imports only the SDK surface (boundary gate enforced in CI); an exemplar consumer builds a minimal custom PM CLI using only @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk; SDK reference docs cover every promoted primitive; zero CLI behavior change (full test suite green)
The pm SDK delivers the complete baseline + primitives of a universal PM tool; the CLI is 100% built on it
Universal customization: any project can assemble its own PM tool from SDK primitives; CLI/MCP/SDK can never drift because they share one implementation

Today the SDK covers extension/package authoring (compose/define/runtime/testing) but core primitives (item store, search, history, governance) are src/core internals reachable only through the CLI; the simplification epic (pm-n7rr) needs a stable primitive layer to shim against

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Gap analysis 2026-07-05 — src/sdk currently exports extension/package-authoring surfaces only (compose, define, runtime, testing, cli-contracts, profile presets/plan). The PM primitives themselves live in src/core (item, store, search, history, governance, schema, config, session, checkpoint, lock) and src/cli/commands imports them directly; ~60 command modules under src/cli/commands also embed business logic beyond parsing/rendering. Consequence; an SDK-only consumer today cannot create, query, or govern items without shelling out to the CLI.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Promotion strategy — domain-by-domain (lifecycle, query, annotations, governance, schema/config/profile) with a zero-behavior-change bar per domain; boundary gate (pm-8778) starts as an allowlist ratchet at current import state so it can merge before promotions finish and tighten with each one. Exemplar package (pm-cbwg) is the acceptance proof for the universality story pm-w89s.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: The CLI-simplification epic pm-n7rr and this epic reinforce each other; consolidated commands should be implemented as thin SDK calls, so the inventory task pm-lodl deliberately feeds both epics and should run before either implementation stream starts.

patch.value:
## Charter
Make the SDK the single public API of pm. Ordering: (1) inventory the CLI-to-core call graph, (2) promote primitives domain-by-domain into src/sdk with stable typed signatures, (3) flip CLI handlers to consume the SDK, (4) enforce the boundary with a lint/CI gate so it can never regress, (5) prove universality with an SDK-only exemplar consumer.

## Layering target
- src/core: private implementation detail (no stability promise)
- src/sdk: the ONLY public programmatic surface — re-exports/wraps core with typed, documented, semver-meaningful contracts
- src/cli + src/mcp: presentation layers that import exclusively from src/sdk
- packages/*: already consume the SDK; gain access to the same primitives the CLI uses

## Non-goals
- No behavior changes to any command (pure re-layering)
- No new storage formats
- Extension/package authoring SDK (compose/define/runtime/testing) stays as-is; this epic adds the missing primitive layer beside it

## Relationship to CLI simplification epic pm-n7rr
pm-n7rr consolidates the command surface; this epic supplies the primitives those consolidated commands call. Inventory (child task) feeds both.

## Promotion index update (audit 2026-07-05, second pass)
Gap-check against the full 60+ command surface found three families uncovered by the original five promotion items; filed: pm-4a7m (history-stream maintenance: redact/repair/compact), pm-je50 (plan workflow primitives), pm-oslr (execution & diagnostics: test running + test-run lifecycle, search eval harness, telemetry stats/export). With these, every CLI command family maps to exactly one SDK promotion item; the pm-lodl inventory validates the partition.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Progress evidence from child items pm-lodl/pm-8778: SDK-first baseline is now explicit and enforceable. Current private boundary debt is quantified at 857 unique src/core import edges across 91 boundary files (101 scanned) from src/cli.ts, src/cli/**, and src/mcp/**. The static quality gate now prevents growth and stale baseline entries, giving each SDK primitive-promotion lane a measurable ratchet target while preserving the project philosophy 'project management = context management'.

author: codex-sdk-agent

patch.value: Public SDK exposes item lifecycle, query/read, annotation/link, governance/validation, schema/config/profile, and history primitives with typed results; src/cli.ts, src/cli/**, src/mcp.ts, and src/mcp/** cannot grow private src/core imports beyond the SDK boundary ratchet baseline enforced in CI and must shrink toward SDK-only imports as primitives are promoted; an exemplar consumer builds a minimal custom PM CLI using only @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk; SDK reference docs cover every promoted primitive; zero CLI behavior change (full test suite green)

message: Align SDK boundary acceptance criteria with CLI and MCP ratchet scope

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-06 audit #7: first execution primitive is LIVE — pm-xzhz shipped src/sdk/runtime.ts (PmClient/runAction) with package export ./sdk/runtime, MCP now dispatches through the SDK runner, and the sdk-import-boundary baseline shrank (PR #456, merged 90caa2a7). ADR pm-muhw ACCEPTED (closed 2026-07-05). Remaining promotion partition (rjqr/98cz/zwpp/3mna/oxrw/4a7m/je50/oslr) still covers all 64 live CLI commands; the 18 new dogfood items filed today are presentation-layer (output shape, help, parsing) and do not alter the partition. Note for implementers: runAction gives domain promotions a reference semantics oracle — each narrow primitive (e.g. sdk list/get) must match the equivalent runAction result shape.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Planning evidence 2026-07-06/07 codex-agent: SDK-first coverage remains canonical here, not a new duplicate epic. Domain promotion items cover query/read (pm-rjqr), lifecycle (pm-98cz), annotations/links (pm-zwpp), schema/config/profile/init (pm-3mna), history maintenance (pm-4a7m), plan workflows (pm-je50), execution/diagnostics (pm-oslr), package/extension lifecycle (pm-x6jf), governance/health (pm-oxrw), docs (pm-ds3b), exemplar CLI (pm-cbwg), and boundary capstone (pm-9x6e). Recommended order: finish typed public primitives before command consolidation flips handlers, then make CLI and MCP pure presentation layers over SDK calls; every primitive needs SDK docs, tests, and behavior parity against existing CLI results.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Promotion-partition progress reconciled against shipped work (PR #477, commit f7e2ac21): pm-rjqr query/read primitives and pm-98cz item-lifecycle primitives are CLOSED with evidence — PmClient + top-level SDK exports now cover list/get/search/context/next/aggregate/stats and create/update/close/claim/release/copy/delete/restore/focus/start-task/pause-task/close-task, including structured create payloads (pm-395t) and published SDK package-coordinate docs (pm-25d0). Remaining open promotion domains: pm-zwpp annotations/links, pm-je50 plan workflow, pm-4a7m history-stream maintenance, pm-3mna schema/config/profile/init, pm-oxrw governance/health/maintenance, pm-oslr execution/diagnostics, pm-x6jf package/extension lifecycle — then capstone pm-9x6e (zero the 91-entry import-boundary baseline) and exemplar pm-cbwg.

author: codex-agent
message: Include SDK package-diagnostics contract slice in active multi-item PR bundle

author: codex-agent
message: Link SDK-first architecture progress to isolated package diagnostics PR item

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implementation slice update: pm-6abs now contributes to the SDK-first architecture lane by exposing isolated package/extension doctor flags through CLI help, completions, SDK contracts, and MCP/tool schema while keeping extension API method calls safe for package activators that destructure the public API.

value.text: Post-review evidence: addressed CodeRabbit/Gemini/Cubic findings by correcting SDK docs files/docs examples to flat add arrays with top-level note, widening the generic schema() SDK return to SchemaResult, requiring names for show/lint SDK helpers, and stamping closed_at in the direct update mutation helper path. Verification passed: pnpm typecheck; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/sdk/sdk-context-primitives.spec.ts tests/unit/commands/update-command.spec.ts; pnpm build; pnpm lint; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (100/100/100/100); pnpm quality:docs-skills; PM_AUTHOR=claude-code-agent pm health --check-only --summary --json; PM_AUTHOR=claude-code-agent pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift --json (ok with existing validate_resolution_missing_fields:269 warning); temp npm-pack installed SDK proof; pnpm changelog:pm:check with pm-changelog 2026.7.6 unchanged.

value.author: codex
value.text: CI follow-up evidence: Gates static on PR head 4b09d2e1 failed because the Greptile evidence comment was committed after the package-owned CHANGELOG.md generation, making the closed-item changelog input newer than the file. Fix is to regenerate CHANGELOG.md after this final pm evidence mutation and avoid further tracker mutations before the next push.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: SDK-first frontier 2026-07-10: read/query (pm-rjqr) and lifecycle-mutation (pm-98cz) promotions SHIPPED (closed 2026-07-07). With pm-lodl (call-graph inventory) and ADR pm-muhw closed, the entire remaining promotion partition is now UNBLOCKED and actionable: pm-zwpp (annotations/links), pm-3mna (schema/config/profile/init), pm-oxrw (governance/validate/health/maintenance), pm-4a7m (history-stream maintenance), pm-je50 (plan workflow), pm-oslr (execution/diagnostics). Critical path to the exemplar-package proof pm-cbwg runs through zwpp+3mna+oxrw (its two other blockers rjqr/98cz are already closed); pm-ds3b (full-partition umbrella) needs all six. Universality story pm-w89s waits on 3mna/oxrw/zwpp. Semantics oracle per the 07-06 note still applies: each promoted primitive must match the equivalent runAction result shape.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Frontier update 2026-07-10 (evening): S1–S4 implementation slicing notes now exist on ALL six unblocked promotion slices — pm-zwpp, pm-3mna, pm-oxrw, pm-4a7m, pm-je50, pm-oslr. Every slice has a grounded already-public inventory, line-counted gap, ordered sub-slices with estimates, and fix-site coordination links. Any agent can enter any slice cold. Recommended entry order across the epic (universality-weighted): 3mna S1 (schema, gates pm-w89s) -> zwpp S1 (annotation kernel, 3 facets per slice) -> oxrw -> 4a7m -> je50 -> oslr (P3, tooling depth). Line-count totals: 3mna ~5.1k, oslr ~4.8k, oxrw ~5.1k-territory, je50 ~1.8k single-module, zwpp ~1.5k, 4a7m per its note.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
SDK developer-experience map (2026-07-11, maintainer directive: drastically improve pm SDK DX + SDK must be extensible enough to build a full VCS or a company's entire business logic on it; documentation-only pass).

SDK DX = a consumer builds real systems WITHOUT shelling to the CLI and without reading pm-cli source. The promotion partition (zwpp/3mna/oxrw/4a7m/je50/oslr, all sliced S1-S4) IS the DX program — every un-promoted domain is a DX hole because the only workaround is CLI subprocess + envelope parsing. Nothing new to file; this note records the DX bar each promoted domain must clear:
1. Typed results, no envelope archaeology — SDK returns domain objects, never CLI-shaped envelopes; the runAction semantics oracle (07-06 note) guarantees parity without inheriting CLI output quirks.
2. Errors as values with stable codes — the pm-g9xk lean-error work should define the code table ONCE in the SDK; CLI/MCP render it (no CLI-only error vocabulary).
3. Every capability reachable in-process — acceptance probe stays pm-cbwg (exemplar package) + pm-w89s (full custom PM tool, SDK-only).
4. Extension-author DX — sdk/testing.ts harness (runRegistered*ForTest family) is the precedent; each promoted domain ships its test-harness counterpart in the same PR, or authors fall back to e2e-only testing.

UNIVERSALITY DIRECTIVE (re-affirmed by maintainer 2026-07-11): building a git-like VCS or company business workflows on pm SDK primitives is an explicit product goal, not an aspiration. Proof stories already exist and are fenced — pm-8ngt (platform-developer story), pm-xtrd (VCS-style changeset exemplar spike), pm-w89s (integrator story). Primitive checklist the VCS build exercises, all with existing owners: custom schema/types (3mna), event-sourced immutable history + replay (klo8/hib1/3z0k territory), hooks/extension points (loader + compose, shipped), query predicates (plwm), concurrent claims/locking (dj98 races bgcu/khdq/zwib), governance/policy (xc68). Gap-check on 8ngt/xtrd happens AFTER the six slices land — the spike is the honest detector of missing primitives; do not speculative-file SDK features ahead of it.
DOCS DEBT (recorded, not filed — bundling evidence first per numbers-first rule): SDK entry-point docs are thinner than command docs; the cbwg exemplar doubles as the tutorial narrative when it lands.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Frontier update 2026-07-11 (post-PR#512): pm-je50 (plan) is the FIRST of the six unblocked promotion slices to ship — full governed plan workflow now public via typed PmClient.plan(...) (contracts 4.0.7, docs/SDK.md, tarball-consumer proof). Shipped out of recommended entry order; remaining order unchanged: 3mna S1 -> zwpp S1 -> oxrw -> 4a7m -> oslr. Note for zwpp implementers: je50 S2 annotation shapes shipped ahead of the zwpp S1 annotation kernel — zwpp S1 should reconcile with the shipped plan annotation primitives rather than invent a parallel engine (inverse of the original coordination note on pm-je50). Downstream unchanged: cbwg needs zwpp+3mna+oxrw; ds3b umbrella now 1/6 done; w89s waits on 3mna/oxrw/zwpp.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-12 frontier constraint: sdk/runtime.ts is at 3388/3400 effective lines (static gate cap) — PR#512/#517/#520 each grew it. Any next slice (3mna S1 is the entry) that adds runtime actions will FAIL the file-length gate. The decomposition seam is owned by pm-yh8r (per-domain action tables + shared dispatch kernel, byte-identical runAction semantics); land it as a prep PR or as the first commit of 3mna S1. Do not raise the 3400 cap to dodge this.
